headline: Why the second round of France's municipal elections matters
subhead: The vote on Sunday, March 22, will provide valuable national insights, with a focus on the far-right's result, the impact of mergers or divisions within the left and right and the right's gamble to stay in the race.

headline: Iranian missiles sow panic in Israeli town
Former FBI director Robert Mueller, who investigated Russia-Trump campaign ties, dies
Cuba hit by second nationwide blackout in a week
subhead: Cuba's national power network failed for the second time in less than a week, leaving streets pitch black and forcing people to navigate using flashlights. Blackouts have intensified because of aging infrastructure and a US oil blockade.

headline: The UAE is reorganizing its arms supply network for Sudanese paramilitaries
subhead: Abu Dhabi is seeking to develop new logistical routes from Sudan's neighboring countries, such as Ethiopia and the Central African Republic, to support the Rapid Support Forces.

headline: London exhibit highlights a meeting between two major suffragettes
subhead: The Florence Nightingale Museum, named after the nurse who elevated her profession and founded the first school dedicated to nursing, is hosting an exhibition devoted to the famous suffragette Emmeline Pankhurst.

headline: Michael Rider, Celine designer: 'Bringing joy and humor to fashion doesn't mean making funny clothes'
subhead: Since taking over as creative director of Celine in October 2024, the American designer, who succeeded Phoebe Philo and Hedi Slimane, has seized upon the brand's core principles, infusing them with his own positive and personal touch.

subhead: The commissioner-general of the United Nations agency for Palestinian refugees, whose term ends on March 31, voices deep concern for the survival of the underfunded organization that has destabilized by Israeli attacks and disregard for international law.

headline: Decision to award AFCON 2025 victory to Morocco further damages the credibility of African football
subhead: Since stripping Senegal of the continental championship title, ruling the team had 'forfeited,' the Confédération Africaine de Football (CAF) has faced intense criticism over its ability to manage competitions.
